Taking into account that this movie takes place in an alternate universe, it is a great reboot of Star Trek. Full of action, drama, humor and surprises, I feel that it was a nice homage to the original series. I've watched it at least twice, years apart, and it made me feel like an 11 year old, watching OG Star Trek reruns with my dad.

Star Trek (with Chris Pine) successfully revitalizes the franchise, delivering a fun, engaging, and action-packed experience. Chris Pine steps in as the new leader with charisma and confidence, providing a fresh energy that propels the story forward while honoring the essence of the original series. The action sequences are thrilling and well-choreographed, keeping viewers invested throughout, and the director does an excellent job balancing these moments with character development. Each main character receives attention, including backstory and emotional depth, which helps audiences connect with the crew. The screenplay is generally strong, effectively updating the franchise for a modern audience while maintaining the core Star Trek spirit. Minor missteps exist, but they do not detract from the overall experience. Pine’s performance is particularly noteworthy, portraying a leader who is both heroic and relatable, making this iteration of Star Trek highly enjoyable.
Action packed and fun, but generic and lacking of any meaning, which is a Star trek staple. Time traveling plots always suck, and the villain looks cheap.

As a movie, I don’t think it’s that good. It gave me a Transformers cinematography vibe. Made sense when I saw JJ Abrams come up on the screen. However, I think it’s still a fun movie. Despite characters developing rapidly, it made me want to watch the old Star Trek series (I didn’t realize there were so many) and understand what real Sci-Fi from the 60s-80s was like. My wife loved it because of all the gnarly monsters and big explosions.

I hadn't seen much of the show before I saw this movie, and it pulled me in like a black hole!! The story is gripping, the characters are great, and (as always) Michael Giacchino kills it with the music!!!
Awesome movie both acting and visuals. Throwback and nostalgic with a modern twist. Even the comedy breaks made this movie familiar!
In future startrek movies..please slow down the action as not to have to use your slow motion to comprehend. Also, not every action scene need full on philharmonic orchestra.
Boring, flattened and sometimes stupid. The opening is very slow and the other Spock just feel weird.

The future begins. Star Trek is a 2009 American science fiction action film directed by J. J. Abrams 🛸 It’s very good! 😀 I’d definitely recommend it 👍🏼 The fate of the galaxy rests in the hands of bitter rivals. One, James Kirk, is a delinquent, thrill-seeking Iowa farm boy. The other, Spock, a Vulcan, was raised in a logic-based society that rejects all emotion. As fiery instinct clashes with calm reason, their unlikely but powerful partnership is the only thing capable of leading their crew through unimaginable danger, boldly going where no one has gone before. The human adventure has begun again.

Rebooting the classic iteration of Star Trek is done to perfection. The crew of the Enterprise maintains their long-standing characteristics while possessing a few modern eccentricities. The casting is also flawless, especially Zachary Quinto as Spock, whose performance nails the mannerisms of Leonard Nimoy's timeless portrayal of the character. The special effects are wondrous to boot, given that the technology has caught up with the genre. This spacefaring adventure has heart, drama, and thrills.
A crowd-pleasing reboot that honors Star Trek’s adventurous spirit while giving it blockbuster energy. Not perfect, but a stellar reintroduction to the final frontier.

A brilliant reboot with a great cast. Maybe a bit much lens flare. :)

There are some minor plot conviences that could easily be fixed with sharper more refined writing and this has quite a bit more action and "modern" pacing to it more than any other Star Trek film to this point. But it's so well made and does such a great job doing everything it sets out to do that it's easy to overlook itI think for most people. Even if you haven't seen the films before this you don't even need to, it's that good. Simliar to how Wrath of Khan you don't need to have seen the first or the show to see how good it is. This also does an amazing job serving as a prequel and reboot to the franchise as well without being disrespectful to the other entries. Music is really good and very well used. I do wish it was a bit more catchy on it's own though. The Beastie Boys ironically never feels out of place. Cinematography is really good. It defintely doesn't feel like a TV Series and understands it's a big screen flick. It also does a very good job nailing the Star Trek look while modernizing and standing out on it's own. I will say there is ALOT of CGI in this and lots of lens flares to. But it's really well done and looks good. Everything else is pretty masterful aside from the minor plot conviences. It does a great job balancing action, humor, heart, and character with a great story and being respectful to longtime fans. This is easily JJ Abrams finest hour. Everyone should give this a try once. All Time Classic.

A riveting prequel/reboot with enough lens flares to make Spielberg salivate, this tightly bundled sci-fi action/adventure fare reintroduces it’s franchise to a new generation in ways that other properties can only dream of (cough—Star Wars—cough), with ahead of their time visuals, a picture perfect cast, rapid-fire yet smooth pacing and unexpectedly strong narrative parallels.
